title: Regalia
content:
If the rich are different
        they show it with the
        clarity of their table
        as Fitzgerald decried,
        the breathless hush
        of their garden regalias,
        the manner in which wedgewood
        crystal are cleaned to a
        polished exactness - 
        the shimmer of expensive china
        no less repetitive than
        the hulking boys
        waiting in window stops,
        monsoon rain pelting
        the upper Punjab plains.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Paul Cameron Brown, titled "Regalia", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
